This generated package must cover the full detailed-PRD behavior represented by all authoritative public test case files, not just a subset mentioned in the fuzzy PRD. Public feature files: feature10_nested_object_tool_inputs, feature11_array_tool_inputs, feature12_array_of_object_tool_inputs, feature13_tool_definition_validation, feature1_static_resource_listing, feature2_static_resource_pagination, feature3_static_resource_reading, feature4_static_resource_definition_validation, feature5_template_resource_reading, feature6_template_resource_listing, feature7_simple_tool_schema_and_call, feature8_tool_pagination, feature9_multi_argument_tool_calls. Feature descriptions include: A tool input may be a required object with its own required fields and optional fields.; Create a user with details; ; Username; Email address; Age; Create a user with details; ; Username; Email address; Age; Create a user with details; ; Username; Email address; Age; Create a user with details;

The execution adapter must accept exactly the input shapes used by public_test_cases and emit stdout exactly equal to each expected_output. Observed top-level input fields/values include: setup=['{dict}'], request=['{dict}']. Do not require a different discriminator field, different action names, JSON output where line output is expected, or extra PASS/FAIL metadata.

The embedded public_test_cases are part of oracle_data and are authoritative for exact input shapes, action/operation names, stdout line order, whitespace, error categories, numeric formatting, and deterministic serialization. If prose, fuzzy_prd, context pointers, or older hidden constraints are vague or conflict with expected_output, the public_test_cases expected_output is the pass-critical contract.

Ignore any vague analogy or context pointer that names an unrelated module, pipeline, login flow, audit generator, or previous project. Such wording is non-normative; only this repo’s detailed PRD, FULL_SCOPE, EXECUTION_CONTRACT, and public_test_cases define the task.

context_pointers.vague_reference: include schema descriptors as nested objects
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The tool objects must contain the literal strings 'inputSchema', 'type', 'properties', and 'required' as nested JSON field names representing the OpenAPI structure.
When a tool call succeeds, the response 'content' array must contain a string literal suffix of 'text' representing the successful output type indicator.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_nested_object_tool_inputs.json.description: A tool input may be a required object with its own required fields and optional fields.

feature11_array_tool_inputs.json.description: A tool input may be an array of primitive values and validates each element by index.

feature12_array_of_object_tool_inputs.js.description: A tool input may be an array of objects whose elements validate nested required fields.

feature13_tool_definition_validation.jso.description: Invalid tool definitions are rejected with normalized error categories.

feature1_static_resource_listing.json.description: Registered static resources are listed with their URI, display name, description, and default MIME type.

feature2_static_resource_pagination.json.description: Static resources preserve registration order and support cursor-based pagination.

feature3_static_resource_reading.json.description: Reading a registered static resource returns the requested URI, MIME type, and text content; unknown URIs are reported neutrally.

feature4_static_resource_definition_vali.description: Invalid static resource definitions are rejected with normalized error categories.

feature5_template_resource_reading.json.description: Template resources match concrete URIs, extract path variables, and render content from those variables.

feature6_template_resource_listing.json.description: Registered resource templates are listed with template URI, display name, description, and MIME type, and support pagination.

feature7_simple_tool_schema_and_call.jso.description: A tool with one required text input exposes a JSON-compatible input schema and returns text content when called.

feature8_tool_pagination.json.description: Tool listings preserve registration order and expose next cursors when a page has more entries.

feature9_multi_argument_tool_calls.json.description: Tools may declare multiple required and optional inputs; missing required inputs return normalized validation errors.

_constraints.output_rule: Returns a normalized error if validation fails
Returns a normalized error if validation fails
Returns array of registered static resources in registration order with explicit MIME type
Returns up to 'page_size' items; includes 'nextCursor' if total items exceed page size
Returns resources starting from index equal to parsed integer value of cursor

_constraints.input_condition: request target is 'resources' but action is not 'list'
_constraints.output_rule: Not explicitly defined for other actions, but implies standard listing behavior or error if 'list' is missing for basic listing
exact_values: No specific rule provided in test cases for non-list actions
Action must be 'list' for successful resource catalog listing

_constraints.input_condition: setup.resources contains a static resource with uri matching request.read.uri
_constraints.output_rule: Returns contents array with uri, mimeType, and text fields

_constraints.input_condition: setup.resources does not contain a static resource matching request.read.uri
_constraints.output_rule: Returns error object
Returns contents array with uri, mimeType, and computed text

_constraints.input_condition: request.action == 'read' and no matching template or static resource
_constraints.output_rule: Returns error object
Returns array of tools with nested inputSchema
Schema output for that property includes 'type': 'string'

_constraints.input_condition: Tool argument type is 'array' with 'integer' items
_constraints.output_rule: Schema output includes 'type': 'array', 'items': { 'type': 'integer' }
Schema output nests the 'properties' object under the argument property name

_constraints.input_condition: Tool argument is an 'object' with 'required' fields
_constraints.output_rule: Schema output includes 'required' array listing exactly the names of fields with required: true
Returns envelope with 'content' and 'isError'

_constraints.input_condition: Invocation of a tool via 'call' action succeeds
_constraints.output_rule: Returns JSON with 'content' array containing text output and 'isError': false
Includes 'nextCursor' field containing the index of the first item on the next page

_constraints.input_condition: page_size is not explicitly provided in request
_constraints.output_rule: Includes 'nextCursor' field if total resources > 0

_constraints.input_condition: Tool call arguments do not match inputSchema required fields
_constraints.output_rule: Returns isError: true in response
